titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Wesley Brown Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 14, 2021 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Wesley Brown papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Wesley Brown papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wesley Brown, born 1945, received his M.A. in Literature and Creative Writing at The City College of the City University of New York and his B.A. in History and Political Science at State University of New York, Oswego. Wesley Brown is a novelist, playwright, and teacher. He worked with the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party in 1965 and became a member of the Black Panther Party in 1968. In 1972, he was sentenced to three years in prison for refusing induction into the armed services and spent 18 months in Lewisburg Federal Penitentiary. Brown was a professor at Rutgers University (1979-2005). He has taught literature at Bard College at Simon's Rock since 2007.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1960-2010. II. Legal Documents, 1970-1973. III. Guest Books, 2004-2005.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Wesley Brown papers consists of original and photocopied material related to the writer Wesley Brown. The papers primarily contain the creative writing of Wesley Brown, including novels, plays, poems, short stories, film scripts, correspondence, and reviews related to these writings. The papers also include legal documents related to Wesley Brown's refusal to be inducted into the armed services in 1972.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

did.unittitle: Writings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into seven subseries: Essays, Novels, Diaries, Plays, Poetry, Short Stories, Film Scripts, and Reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains the creative writings of Wesley Brown, including novels, plays, poems, short stories, film scripts, correspondence, and reviews related to these writings.

did.unittitle: Essays

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Jes' Grew: Newsletter of the National Association of Third World Writers"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains two issues of the Jes' Grew Newsletter from Fall/Winter 1982 and March 1982.

did.unittitle: "Which Sound Are You On?"
"The Mojo and the Sayso: Myth and Ritual in the work of Larry Neal"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains 2 typescripts and a photocopy of the essay "The Mojo and the Sayso". The file also contains the first volume of The Journal, in which "The Mojo and the Sayso" was initially published.

did.unittitle: Imagining America: Stories from the Promised Land, printed version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Imagining America: Stories from the Promised Land is a multicultural anthology of American fiction from 1900 to 1991. It was edited by Wesley Brown and Amy Ling.

did.unittitle: Why magazine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains 7 issues of Why magazine from March 1970 to November 1971

did.unittitle: "Famous People in Negro History, 1960 Calendar"
"Vietnam Journal" and related writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains 4 edited typescripts of "Vietnam Journal", a manuscript essay titled "What do Black People know about Vietnam?" and the article "Reflections on Vietnam" published in Essence in 1998

did.unittitle: "A Visit to Buchenwald"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains 4 edited typscripts of "A Visit to Buchenwald" and related correspondence.

did.unittitle: "Handwritten Essay re: Dick" (transcribed)
Essays and interviews regarding adoptive son, Anthony

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file contains a typescript essay, an interview with Wesley Brown regarding adopting a child as a single parent, and a photocopy of the essay "Say Brother".

arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains legal documents related to Wesley Brown's refusal to be inducted into the armed services in 1972.

did.unittitle: Defendant Appellant's Brief, Appendix on Appeal

Guest Books

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains two guest books from Wesley Brown's retirement.
